subtitle: A Forest Service cove at the end of an unmarked, one-lane canyon road, where garnet washing off a 1,600-foot ridge streaks the sand purple and the December sun fires through a sea arch for a few days a year.
spike_statement: Garnet eroding from the schist of Pfeiffer Ridge streaks Pfeiffer Beach purple, and near the winter solstice the setting sun lines up through Keyhole Arch.
